{
  "decision": "fail",

  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Single-wall vent pipe installed with insufficient clearance to combustible materials",
    "Vent configuration does not meet minimum clearance requirements for single-wall venting",
    "Potential fire hazard due to heat transfer to adjacent combustible surfaces or construction"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Measure actual clearance from single-wall vent to all combustible materials",
    "Replace single-wall vent with proper Type B double-wall vent or install listed clearance reduction methods per manufacturer and code",
    "Verify and correct vent routing, support, and termination to comply with IRC M1801 and NFPA 54",
    "Inspect surrounding combustible materials for heat damage and replace if necessary",
    "Re-inspect and document compliance before returning furnace to service"
  ],
  "rationale": "Single-wall venting requires a substantial minimum clearance to combustibles, typically 6 inches, which is greater than that required for listed double-wall or Type B vent systems. Insufficient clearance is a known fire and safety hazard, violates IRC M1801 and NFPA 54, and must be corrected before the installation can be considered compliant or safe.",
  "references": [
    "IRC M1801 - Vent systems",
    "NFPA 54 - National Fuel Gas Code"
  ]
}
```
